title: Review: Today Brief History
description: Review the complete implementation: APIClient getBriefs(), BriefDetailView, BriefCardView, TodayView refactor, pagination, fallback logic, unit tests, and documentation. Verify spec compliance, Liquid Glass conventions, accessibility labels on interactive elements (≥44×44 pt tap targets), no hardcoded hex, correct Task cancellation handling, and absence of memory leaks or race conditions in the pagination guard.
acceptance_criteria: All spec-9f85467c acceptance criteria are demonstrably met
No hardcoded hex colors or non-semantic font styles anywhere in the feature
Accessibility labels present on BriefCardView NavigationLinks
Fallback logic is correct and handles 404 without surfacing an error to the user
isLoadingMore guard prevents race conditions on rapid scroll
Code review approved with no blocking findings

handoff_summary.completed: Reviewed all 6 changed files. Corrective bead fixed the two blocking/minor findings: (1) BriefCardView.swift:33 now has .accessibilityLabel combining headline, dateLine, and topicCountLine — VoiceOver picks this up through the NavigationLink wrapper; (2) TodayView.swift:38 empty state now reads "No briefs yet". All other implementation details from the prior review pass remain correct and unchanged.
handoff_summary.remaining: Nothing. All acceptance criteria are met.
handoff_summary.next_action: Bead can be marked complete. No further review or corrective work required.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. Corrective changes are contained to BriefCardView.swift (.accessibilityLabel modifier) and TodayView.swift (empty state string) with no shared state or type changes.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Placing .accessibilityLabel on BriefCardView's root VStack (rather than on the NavigationLink in TodayView) is the correct SwiftUI pattern — the link inherits the label from its content view, giving VoiceOver a single coherent description of the interactive row without duplicating state into TodayView.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: No test changes were required for the corrective fixes (accessibility labels are not unit-testable via XCTest; empty state copy is UI-only). Existing BriefModelTests.swift coverage remains sufficient for the changed surface area.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: BriefDetailView accepts onRefresh but TodayView passes nil — pull-to-refresh in the detail view is a no-op. Intentional per spec; parameter reserved for future use.

conflict_risks: None. Corrective changes are contained to BriefCardView.swift (.accessibilityLabel modifier) and TodayView.swift (empty state string) with no shared state or type changes.

execution_history.summary: Review found 2 findings: 1 blocking (no explicit accessibility labels on NavigationLinks in TodayView, required by acceptance criteria) and 1 minor (empty state copy "No Brief Today" should be "No briefs yet" per spec). All other criteria — fallback logic, isLoadingMore race guard, no hex colors, semantic fonts, pagination, tests — pass.
Created corrective bead B-4a862cf8-corrective for blocked issue
Requeued blocked bead after corrective bead B-4a862cf8-corrective completed
Re-review pass: both prior findings resolved — accessibility label added to BriefCardView root view (propagates to NavigationLink), empty state copy corrected to "No briefs yet". No new findings.

guardrails.template_text:
# Review Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Inspect code, tests, docs, and acceptance criteria for correctness, completeness, and risk.

Allowed actions:
- Review only the changed files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read unrelated files.
- Validate acceptance criteria against the implementation and handoff state.
- Block with a clear recommendation when the bead actually requires implementation work.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before reviewing any files, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries to inform the review; skip entries that don't apply.

Do **not** write to memory — review agents are read-only.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.
- Focus on correctness, completeness, and risk — not style or formatting.
- Keep the review concise. If there are no findings, say so and approve promptly.

Disallowed actions:
- Implement feature work, tests, or docs instead of reporting findings.
- Rewrite architecture or silently fix issues discovered during review.
- Mark incomplete work as accepted without evidence.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (reviews always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of the review result.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the review signoff decision: `approved` means the bead can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when no unresolved findings remain.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved finding count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when any required fix remains.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Review findings ordered by severity, or an explicit statement that no findings were discovered.
- Clear blocked handoff details when the task belongs to another agent type.

worker_prompt_context.title: Review: Today Brief History
worker_prompt_context.description: Review the complete implementation: APIClient getBriefs(), BriefDetailView, BriefCardView, TodayView refactor, pagination, fallback logic, unit tests, and documentation. Verify spec compliance, Liquid Glass conventions, accessibility labels on interactive elements (≥44×44 pt tap targets), no hardcoded hex, correct Task cancellation handling, and absence of memory leaks or race conditions in the pagination guard.
acceptance_criteria: All spec-9f85467c acceptance criteria are demonstrably met
No hardcoded hex colors or non-semantic font styles anywhere in the feature
Accessibility labels present on BriefCardView NavigationLinks
Fallback logic is correct and handles 404 without surfacing an error to the user
isLoadingMore guard prevents race conditions on rapid scroll
Code review approved with no blocking findings
worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks: None. The fix is contained to TodayView.swift and/or BriefCardView.swift with no shared state or type changes.

handoff_summary.completed: Reviewed all 6 changed files: APIClient.swift (getBriefs method), BriefCardView.swift (new), BriefDetailView.swift (new), TodayView.swift (refactored), BriefModelTests.swift (URL + decoding tests), docs/API.md (iOS Today tab section). Validated against spec-9f85467c acceptance criteria and bead acceptance criteria.
handoff_summary.remaining: Developer must add an explicit .accessibilityLabel() to the NavigationLink in TodayView.swift:55 (e.g., derived from brief.headline and dateLine). Optionally fix the empty state copy from "No Brief Today" to "No briefs yet".
handoff_summary.risks: Missing accessibility label is a CLAUDE.md hard requirement (every interactive element needs an accessibility label) and an explicit bead acceptance criterion. The empty-state copy mismatch is minor but semantically inaccurate for the no-briefs-at-all case.
handoff_summary.next_action: Create a defect bead to add .accessibilityLabel() to the NavigationLink in TodayView.swift:55 and fix the empty state copy. Assign to developer agent.
handoff_summary.block_reason: Finding 1 (BLOCKING): TodayView.swift:55 NavigationLink wrapping BriefCardView has no .accessibilityLabel() modifier. Bead acceptance criterion explicitly requires accessibility labels on BriefCardView NavigationLinks; CLAUDE.md requires every interactive element to have one. Fix: add .accessibilityLabel("\(brief.headline ?? "Daily Brief"), \(dateLine), \(topicCountLine)") — or equivalent — on the NavigationLink or BriefCardView's root view.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. The fix is contained to TodayView.swift and/or BriefCardView.swift with no shared state or type changes.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Destination-based NavigationLink (avoids first-tap SwiftUI bug) confirmed correct. isLoadingMore guard is race-condition-free (check+set are synchronous on main actor before first await). isFallback explicit boolean is cleaner than inferring from nextCursor==nil. refreshBriefs() correctly avoids .loading phase flash.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: BriefModelTests.swift covers: getBriefs URL with/without cursor (percent-encoding verified), PaginatedResponse<Brief> decoding with optional fields and nested topics, BriefRef internal/external variants, DateDisplay.mediumDate with fractional seconds and nil/garbage inputs. Coverage is appropriate for the changed surface area.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: BriefDetailView accepts onRefresh but TodayView passes nil — pull-to-refresh in the detail view is a no-op. This is intentional (detail shows a pre-loaded snapshot; spec does not require detail-view refresh) and the parameter remains for future use.

last_agent_result.summary: Re-review pass: both prior findings resolved — accessibility label added to BriefCardView root view (propagates to NavigationLink), empty state copy corrected to "No briefs yet". No new findings.
